behaviorsAudited: renders agent activity, transcript, progress, status, and terminal states without requiring a stale local registry entry
keeps slash-command agent menus and workbench agent surfaces mutually exclusive for input ownership
maps daemon agent lifecycle events into stable task rows and workbench rail entries
survives late daemon completion messages after the main prompt has returned
renders startup auth notices without throwing when provider credential helpers report no Anthropic or AgenC account key
keeps PTY startup, prompt visibility, idle detection, and crash detection covered by E2E scenarios
preserves first-seen agent navigation order while keeping completed agents selectable
preserves exact root-input ownership across local, deferred-daemon, and live-daemon paths with bounded admission and origin-bound rollback

edgeCasesAudited: a daemon completion message arriving after the prompt is visible does not crash the TUI
the slash agents menu owns input while open and does not leave the main prompt marker visible
CI startup without Anthropic or AgenC account credentials maps auth lookup failures to a missing-key state instead of throwing
startup status-notice filtering ignores throwing auth key lookups and keeps other notices renderable
an agent with no transcript renders an empty state instead of throwing during surface selection
a completed agent remains selectable in the rail after active streaming stops
rapid task-state synchronization batches keep row identity stable across running and completed statuses
agent rail next and previous navigation wraps through completed agents without resorting by status or start time
first-start, attach, and pre-consumption preparation failures restore only the exact owned input while daemon-gone live proxies avoid text-only retry duplication
deferred input rejects atomically at 512 entries or 16 MiB of serialized retained content and does not advance ownership sequence on rejection

commandResults: PASS: 11 test files passed, 201 tests passed
implementationEvidence: AgentSurface and AgentsRail preserve stable selection, empty-state safety, first-seen ordering, and completed-agent navigation while daemon task synchronization retains stable row identity.
App admits startup, attachment, and skill context as owned batches, commits only after model submission, and restores UI input only when exact rollback proves pre-consumption.
The live daemon session and deferred agenc-main adapter enforce 512-record and 16-MiB retained-content bounds, account for in-flight input, and bind rollback to the exact origin session.
First-start and attach failures exclude rolled-back context from retry, while daemon-gone live proxies never retry the text portion without its context bundle.
The subagent-completion E2E lingers after prompt return to catch late daemon crashes, and the slash-agents E2E verifies exclusive input ownership.
